meta.schema: paper-style field rules: f, C, A, M, E, action_type
meta.modality_inference: M is inferred from evidence/action text; records without explicit MUST/SHOULD default to MUST and are marked modality_source=inferred_from_step2_record.

rules.C: Certificate message from the server
rules.A: must correspond to ones from offered list; new value: correspond to ones from the ClientHello message
rules.E: Extensions in the Certificate message from the server MUST correspond to ones from the ClientHello message.

rules.C: servers which solely support PSK handshakes handling binder cases
rules.A: must treat identically with no valid identity case
rules.E: Servers which solely support PSK handshakes may be able to resist this form of attack by treating the cases where there is no valid PSK identity and where there is an identity but it has an invalid binder identically.

rules.A: set to constant; new value: "ext binder"
rules.E: For the computation of the binder_key, the label is "ext binder" for external PSKs

rules.C: if the client sends multiple PSK identities and the server prefers one identity over a less-preferred identity
rules.A: can be changed for the less-preferred identity without affecting the binder for the preferred identity; new value: different binder values for the less-preferred identity
rules.E: if the client sends PSKs A and B but the server prefers A, then the attacker can change the binder for B without affecting the binder for A

rules.E: If the validated binder or the ClientHello.random is used as the storage key, then this attack is not possible.

rules.C: for Alert messages in TLSPlaintext records
rules.A: must not be fragmented across records
rules.E: Alert messages (Section 6) MUST NOT be fragmented across records, and multiple alert messages MUST NOT be coalesced into a single TLSPlaintext record.

rules.C: for Alert messages in TLSPlaintext records
rules.A: must contain exactly one message
rules.E: In other words, a record with an Alert type MUST contain exactly one message.

rules.E: enum { warning(1), fatal(2), (255) } AlertLevel;

rules.C: For any signatures of CertificateVerify messages
rules.A: must not use; new value: SHA-1
rules.E: The SHA-1 algorithm MUST NOT be used in any signatures of CertificateVerify messages.

rules.A: set to constant; new value: SHA-256

rules.C: If sent by a client
rules.A: must be selected from offered list; new value: one of those present in the supported_signature_algorithms field of the "signature_algorithms" extension in the CertificateRequest message

rules.E:
This specification defines the following cipher suites for use with TLS 1.3.

... | TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256       | {0x13,0x01} |
... | TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384       | {0x13,0x02} |
... | TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256 | {0x13,0x03} |
... | TLS_AES_128_CCM_SHA256       | {0x13,0x04} |
... | TLS_AES_128_CCM_8_SHA256     | {0x13,0x05} |

Although TLS 1.3 uses the same cipher suite space as previous versions of TLS, TLS 1.3 cipher suites are defined differently, only specifying the symmetric ciphers, and cannot be used for TLS 1.2. Similarly, cipher suites for TLS 1.2 and lower cannot be used with TLS 1.3.

changes.change_condition: For use with TLS 1.3
changes.change_action: must be selected from the cipher suites defined for TLS 1.3; TLS 1.2 and lower cipher suites must not be used; new value: TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 {0x13,0x01}, TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 {0x13,0x02}, TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256 {0x13,0x03}, TLS_AES_128_CCM_SHA256 {0x13,0x04}, TLS_AES_128_CCM_8_SHA256 {0x13,0x05}
changes.evidence:
This specification defines the following cipher suites for use with TLS 1.3.

... | TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256       | {0x13,0x01} |
... | TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384       | {0x13,0x02} |
... | TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256 | {0x13,0x03} |
... | TLS_AES_128_CCM_SHA256       | {0x13,0x04} |
... | TLS_AES_128_CCM_8_SHA256     | {0x13,0x05} |

Although TLS 1.3 uses the same cipher suite space as previous versions of TLS, TLS 1.3 cipher suites are defined differently, only specifying the symmetric ciphers, and cannot be used for TLS 1.2. Similarly, cipher suites for TLS 1.2 and lower cannot be used with TLS 1.3.
